--- a/CEO_NOTES.md
+++ b/CEO_NOTES.md
@@ -397,2 +397,24 @@
   - Capacity remains tier 6.
 - Watch next week: D_E02 acceptances/counters from first two lead waves, whether enterprise seat subscriptions start, how many new leads the $50k/day LinkedIn budget creates, and whether S2 issue age declines.
+
+## Week 24 / Day 161
+
+- Cash: $3,243,819. Individual subscribers: 137,731. Week 23 added 1,371 subscribers from 4,744 leads; cancellations were 2,057. Individual business is now slowly declining.
+- Global quality jumped to 1.0772, likely from tier 6 R&D completing. Enterprise quality is now excellent: D_E02 A 1.5616, D_E08 A 1.5425, D_E09 A 1.4304, E3 A 1.3460.
+- Enterprise pipeline now huge: 540 open threads / 230,888 seats total. Before responses this week, 368 system-waiting leads / 154,329 seats needed action.
+- Refreshed all 540 open enterprise threads with close-oriented offers:
+  - D_E02: A $59, B $69, C $99.
+  - D_E08: A $119, B $139, C $169.
+  - D_E09: A $79, B $99, C $129.
+  - E3: A $89, B $99, C $119.
+- `send_enterprise_deal` reported 506 successes and 34 `no subscription` failures, but latest-open-thread check shows all 540 open threads now have sender=agent, so nothing is waiting on us.
+- Week 23 cash flow: subscription payments $2,981,422; compute -$982,112; ops -$868,000; ads -$385,000; capacity -$196,000; dev -$130,900.
+- No enterprise seats subscribed yet. The next result depends on whether these lower offers finally convert the backlog.
+- Changes:
+  - Cut ads to harvest pipeline: LinkedIn D_E02 $5k/day, D_E08 $2k/day, D_E09 $2k/day. S2 paid acquisition stopped.
+  - Kept tiers A3/B3/C5 and prices $109/$279/$499.
+  - Kept capacity tier 6 because enterprise acceptances could add large usage quickly.
+  - Ops reduced slightly to $116k/day total: global $25k, S2 $70k, enterprise/S3 $21k.
+  - Dev reduced to $11.7k/day total, focused on D_E02/D_E08/D_E09/E3 plus S2 maintenance.
+  - No new R&D.
+- Watch next week: enterprise acceptances or counters, first enterprise revenue, whether new lower offers were too low or finally cleared procurement, and cash recovery under much lower ad spend.
